---
title: "Deploying A Project"
---

---
title: "Deploying a Project"
source: "https://support.hyland.com/r/Current/Hyland-Intelligent-Document-Processing-IDP/znk1745947949681"
section: "Hyland Intelligent Document Processing (IDP)"
---

Studio Admin is used to deploy the projects created and released through modeler and manage the processes and tasks created within the deployed applications. Once a released project has been deployed it is referred to as an application.

The Studio Admin application is accessed using the [https://www.experience.hyland.com/environments/](https://www.experience.hyland.com/environments/) URL.

There are three main areas the Admin Application covers:

- A [DevOps section](https://support.hyland.com/access?dita:topicPath=zig1676033888990.dita) used to deploy projects, and manage and upgrade applications.
- A [Process Admin section](https://support.hyland.com/access?dita:topicPath=sao1676038705001.dita) used to manage process instances, tasks and the audit details of all applications.
- An [Application Capacity](https://support.hyland.com/access?dita:topicPath=reu1718357448565.dita) section used to show how many applications can be created and used at the same time.

The DevOps section of the Admin Application is used to deploy projects and manage and upgrade applications.

1.  In the Studio Admin application, navigate to DevOps > Project Releases and search for your project.
2.  Click the ellipsis button adjacent to the project you want to deploy and select Deploy. The Deploy Application dialog box is displayed.   ![](data:image/gif;base64,)
3.  In the Environment list, select the appropriate environment and then click Next. The Validation area is displayed. You do not need to modify the Application Name and Project Release.
4.  Under Validation, ensure that there are no validation errors. Click Next. The Admin Access area is displayed.
5.  Under Admin users, enter names to assign admin users and then click Next.
Repeat the same process to provide Manager access and User access to users.

Note: Make sure the user(s) have the Administrator role assigned for the Studio Admin.
6.  Click Deploy. The application is deployed.

Open DevOps >  Application Instance to check the deployment status of your Application. After your application is deployed, you can test it. On the Application Instances page, click workspace next to your deployed application. Your application opens in Workspace.
